205	## Mathlib dependency anchors (`\mathlibok`)
206	
207	When your chapter's proofs rely on a definition or lemma that **Mathlib already provides** (and the project will not re-prove), it is good practice — not just a bare `\uses{}` to nowhere — to make that dependency a first-class blueprint block: a **Mathlib dependency anchor**. Doing so:

209	- makes the reliance explicit and readable (a mathematician sees exactly which Mathlib result the route stands on);
210	- gives `leandag` a real node, so the `\uses{}` resolves (no broken/unknown ref) and the node's effort is **done** rather than ∞;

213	Write the anchor like any statement block, with three differences:

215	```latex

217	  \label{lem:hensel_mathlib}
218	  \lean{Mathlib.RingTheory.Henselian.foo}   % the REAL Mathlib declaration
219	  \mathlibok

\begin{lemma}
[Ext transport along the whole-scheme spectrum equivalence (Need~\#1)]
  \label{lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.modulesIsoSpecExtTransport}
  % NOTE: the Ext transport uses Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects (NOT a
  % functor-composition lemma for Ext.mapExactFunctor, which is absent from Mathlib); requires
  % [EnoughInjectives U.Modules] supplied at the consumer site.
  \uses{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib, lem:ext_mapExactFunctor_mathlib,
    lem:modules_pushforward_mathlib, lem:jshriek_corepr}
  Let \(U\) be a scheme with \([\,\mathrm{IsAffine}\ U\,]\), let \(V \subseteq U\) be an affine open,
  and let \(\mathcal{H}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module. The whole-scheme isomorphism
  \(U \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_U)\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib})
  induces, for every \(q\), an isomorphism
  \[
    \operatorname{Ext}^q_{U}\bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_V,\, \mathcal{H}\bigr)
      \;\cong\;
    \operatorname{Ext}^q_{\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U}\bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_{V'},\, \mathcal{H}'\bigr),
  \]
  where \(V'\) is the affine open image of \(V\) under the isomorphism and \(\mathcal{H}'\) is the
  transported (still quasi-coherent) coefficient. In particular the residual over \(U\) vanishes iff
  its transport over \(\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U\) does.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib, lem:ext_mapExactFunctor_mathlib,
    lem:modules_pushforward_mathlib, lem:jshriek_corepr}
  Since \(U\) is affine, \(\operatorname{Scheme.isoSpec}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib})
  gives a genuine isomorphism of schemes \(\varphi : U \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_U)\).
  Assemble from the pushforward functors and their coherences
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_pushforward_mathlib}) the equivalence of module categories
  \[
    \Phi : U.\mathrm{Modules} \;\simeq\; (\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U).\mathrm{Modules},
  \]
  with \(\Phi\) the pushforward along \(\varphi.\mathrm{hom}\) and its quasi-inverse the pushforward
  along \(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}\); the unit and counit are supplied by the composition and identity
  coherences. An equivalence is exact, so \(\operatorname{Ext.mapExactFunctor}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_mapExactFunctor_mathlib}) applied to \(\Phi\) and to its quasi-inverse gives
  mutually inverse maps on \(\operatorname{Ext}\), hence the displayed isomorphism. It remains to
  identify the images of the two arguments. The functor \(j_!\mathcal{O}_{(-)}\) is built from the
  Yoneda corepresentability data (Lemma~\ref{lem:jshriek_corepr}) on opens, which a scheme
  isomorphism carries along: \(\Phi(j_!\mathcal{O}_V) \cong j_!\mathcal{O}_{V'}\) for \(V'\) the image
  of \(V\), an affine open of \(\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U\); and \(\Phi(\mathcal{H}) = \mathcal{H}'\)
  is again quasi-coherent because pushforward along an isomorphism preserves quasi-coherence.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Pushforward along an iso commutes with the free functor]
  \label{lem:pushforward_commutes_free}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_commutes_free}
  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
  \uses{def:jshriek_ou}
  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes and write
  \(\Phi = \operatorname{pushforwardEquivOfIso}\varphi : X.\mathrm{Modules} \simeq Y.\mathrm{Modules}\)
  for the induced equivalence of module categories, with \(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}\) the corresponding
  pushforward on presheaves of modules along the homeomorphism underlying \(\varphi\). Then for every
  presheaf of modules \(P\) on \(X\) there is a natural isomorphism
  \[
    \Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\bigl(\operatorname{free} P\bigr)
      \;\cong\;
    \operatorname{free}\bigl(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}} P\bigr),
  \]
  where \(\operatorname{free}\) is the free presheaf-of-modules functor of Definition~\ref{def:jshriek_ou}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:jshriek_ou}
  The free-module functor is the left adjoint of the forgetful functor from presheaves of modules to
  presheaves of abelian groups, and pushforward along the isomorphism \(\varphi\) is itself an
  equivalence that commutes with the forgetful functors --- it relabels opens along the underlying
  homeomorphism and leaves the underlying abelian-group data untouched. A left adjoint composed with an
  equivalence is again a left adjoint, now to the conjugate forgetful functor; the mate of the identity
  comparison between the two forgetful functors is the asserted natural isomorphism
  \(\Phi(\operatorname{free} P) \cong \operatorname{free}(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}} P)\), canonical by
  uniqueness of left adjoints.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Pushforward along an iso commutes with sheafification]
  \label{lem:pushforward_commutes_sheafify}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_commutes_sheafify}
  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
  \uses{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}
  With \(\varphi : X \cong Y\), \(\Phi\) and \(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}\) as above, for every presheaf of
  modules \(Q\) on \(X\) there is a natural isomorphism
  \[
    \Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\bigl(\operatorname{sheafification} Q\bigr)
      \;\cong\;
    \operatorname{sheafification}\bigl(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}} Q\bigr).
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}
  The homeomorphism underlying \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism of sites between the opens of \(X\) and the
  opens of \(Y\), under which covering sieves correspond. Hence the two sheafification adjunctions
  \(\operatorname{sheafification} \dashv \iota\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}) correspond across
  the equivalence: both \(\Phi \circ \operatorname{sheafification}\) and
  \(\operatorname{sheafification} \circ \Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}\) are left adjoint to the common composite of
  the inclusion \(\iota\) with the presheaf transport \(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}^{-1}\), so they agree up to
  the canonical natural isomorphism of left adjoints.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[The representable transports across the homeomorphism]
  \label{lem:yoneda_transport_along_homeo}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.yoneda_transport_along_homeo}
  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
  For a scheme isomorphism \(\varphi : X \cong Y\), whose underlying homeomorphism induces the
  order-isomorphism of frames \(\operatorname{Opens} X \cong \operatorname{Opens} Y\), and any open
  \(V \subseteq X\), the presheaf pushforward of the representable presheaf carries it to the
  representable on the image open:
  \[
    \Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\, V\bigr) \;\cong\; \mathbf{y}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{hom} \,{''}\, V),
  \]
  where \(\varphi.\mathrm{hom} \,{''}\, V\) is the image open of \(V\) in \(Y\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  The presheaf pushforward \(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}\) acts on opens by the inverse-image order-isomorphism
  \(W \mapsto \varphi^{-1}(W)\) of the homeomorphism. Hence
  \((\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}\,\mathbf{y}\,V)(W) = (\mathbf{y}\,V)(\varphi^{-1} W)\) is inhabited exactly when
  \(\varphi^{-1} W \subseteq V\), i.e.\ when \(W \subseteq \varphi.\mathrm{hom}\,{''}\,V\); this is
  precisely \((\mathbf{y}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{hom}\,{''}\,V))(W)\). The bijection is natural in \(W\)
  because the open-image map is monotone and inverts the restriction order, so the two representables
  coincide as presheaves.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[A scheme isomorphism carries the corepresenting object along]
  \label{lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.jShriekOU_transport_along_iso}
  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
  \uses{def:jshriek_ou, lem:pushforward_commutes_free, lem:pushforward_commutes_sheafify,
    lem:yoneda_transport_along_homeo}
  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes, \(V \subseteq X\) an open, and
  \(\Phi = \operatorname{pushforwardEquivOfIso}\varphi : X.\mathrm{Modules} \simeq Y.\mathrm{Modules}\)
  the induced equivalence. Then the pushforward carries the corepresenting object of
  Definition~\ref{def:jshriek_ou} along the isomorphism:
  \[
    \Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_V\bigr)
      \;\cong\;
    j_!\mathcal{O}_{\varphi.\mathrm{hom}\,{''}\,V},
  \]
  where \(\varphi.\mathrm{hom}\,{''}\,V\) is the image open of \(V\) in \(Y\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:jshriek_ou, lem:pushforward_commutes_free, lem:pushforward_commutes_sheafify,
    lem:yoneda_transport_along_homeo}
  Unfold \(j_!\mathcal{O}_V = \operatorname{sheafification}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V))\)
  (Definition~\ref{def:jshriek_ou}) and chase the three commutations. Pushforward commutes with
  sheafification (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_commutes_sheafify}):
  \(\Phi(\operatorname{sheafification}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V))) \cong
  \operatorname{sheafification}(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)))\). It commutes
  with the free functor (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_commutes_free}):
  \(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)) \cong
  \operatorname{free}(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}(\mathbf{y}\,V))\). And the representable transports to the
  representable on the image open (Lemma~\ref{lem:yoneda_transport_along_homeo}):
  \(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}(\mathbf{y}\,V) \cong \mathbf{y}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{hom}\,{''}\,V)\). Composing the
  three gives
  \(\Phi(j_!\mathcal{O}_V) \cong
  \operatorname{sheafification}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{hom}\,{''}\,V))) =
  j_!\mathcal{O}_{\varphi.\mathrm{hom}\,{''}\,V}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Pushforward along an iso preserves quasi-coherence]
  \label{lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh}
  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes and \(H : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) a
  quasi-coherent module. Then the pushforward \(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\) along
  \(\varphi\) is again quasi-coherent.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  Pushforward along the isomorphism of ringed spaces \(\varphi\) is the transport of module data along
  the induced isomorphism of structure-ring sheaves \(X.\operatorname{ringCatSheaf} \cong
  Y.\operatorname{ringCatSheaf}\): it relabels opens by the homeomorphism and the
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-action by the ring-sheaf isomorphism. Quasi-coherence is the local existence of a
  presentation \(\mathcal{O}^{(J)} \to \mathcal{O}^{(I)} \to \mathcal{F} \to 0\) on a cover; such a
  presentation transports verbatim across the iso of ringed spaces --- covers go to covers under the
  homeomorphism and free modules to free modules under the ring-sheaf iso --- so \(\Phi H\) inherits a
  local presentation and is quasi-coherent. No off-the-shelf Mathlib instance supplies this transport;
  it is genuine but bounded infrastructure.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[The image of an affine open under an open immersion is affine]
  \label{lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(f : X \to Y\) be an open immersion of schemes (in particular an isomorphism) and \(U\) an open
  of \(X\). Then the image open \(f \,{''}\, U\) is affine if and only if \(U\) is affine. In
  particular the image \(V'\) of an affine open \(V\) under a scheme isomorphism is an affine open.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}
[Ext transport along the whole-scheme spectrum equivalence (Need~\#1)]
  \label{lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.modulesIsoSpecExtTransport,
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso,
    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive,
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv}
  % NOTE: the Ext transport uses Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects (NOT a
  % functor-composition lemma for Ext.mapExactFunctor, which is absent from Mathlib); requires
  % [EnoughInjectives U.Modules] supplied at the consumer site.
  \uses{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib, lem:ext_mapExactFunctor_mathlib,
    lem:modules_pushforward_mathlib, lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso,
    lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh, lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}
  Let \(U\) be a scheme with \([\,\mathrm{IsAffine}\ U\,]\), let \(V \subseteq U\) be an affine open,
  and let \(\mathcal{H}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module. The whole-scheme isomorphism
  \(U \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_U)\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib})
  induces, for every \(q\), an isomorphism
  \[
    \operatorname{Ext}^q_{U}\bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_V,\, \mathcal{H}\bigr)
      \;\cong\;
    \operatorname{Ext}^q_{\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U}\bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_{V'},\, \mathcal{H}'\bigr),
  \]
  where \(V'\) is the affine open image of \(V\) under the isomorphism and \(\mathcal{H}'\) is the
  transported (still quasi-coherent) coefficient. In particular the residual over \(U\) vanishes iff
  its transport over \(\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U\) does.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib, lem:ext_mapExactFunctor_mathlib,
    lem:modules_pushforward_mathlib, lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso,
    lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh, lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}
  Since \(U\) is affine, \(\operatorname{Scheme.isoSpec}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib})
  gives a genuine isomorphism of schemes \(\varphi : U \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_U)\).
  Assemble from the pushforward functors and their coherences
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_pushforward_mathlib}) the equivalence of module categories
  \[
    \Phi = \operatorname{pushforwardEquivOfIso}\varphi :
      U.\mathrm{Modules} \;\simeq\; (\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U).\mathrm{Modules},
  \]
  with \(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}\) the pushforward along \(\varphi.\mathrm{hom}\) and its quasi-inverse the
  pushforward along \(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}\); the unit and counit are supplied by the composition and
  identity coherences. An equivalence is exact and additive
  (\(\operatorname{pushforwardEquivOfIso\_functor\_additive}\)), so
  \(\operatorname{Ext.mapExactFunctor}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_mapExactFunctor_mathlib}) applies. Because
  Mathlib has no functor-composition lemma for \(\operatorname{Ext.mapExactFunctor}\), the inverse map
  is produced instead from \(\operatorname{Functor.mapExt\_bijective\_of\_preservesInjectiveObjects}\):
  \(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}\), being one half of an equivalence, preserves injective objects, so its
  induced \(\operatorname{Ext}\)-map is bijective, and \(\operatorname{pushforwardExtAddEquiv}\) packages
  it as an additive isomorphism (this is where \([\,\mathrm{EnoughInjectives}\ U.\mathrm{Modules}\,]\) is
  consumed). This gives the displayed isomorphism once the two arguments are identified. For the first
  argument, a scheme isomorphism carries the corepresenting object along
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso}): \(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}(j_!\mathcal{O}_V) \cong
  j_!\mathcal{O}_{V'}\), where \(V' = \varphi.\mathrm{hom}\,{''}\,V\) is the image, an affine open of
  \(\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U\) by Lemma~\ref{lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}. For the second,
  \(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}(\mathcal{H}) = \mathcal{H}'\) is again quasi-coherent because pushforward along
  an isomorphism preserves quasi-coherence (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh}).
\end{proof}

% ----- Change-of-ring chain for the general-affine-open Čech-vanishing seed -----
% Mirror of the localizationAway chain (lem:isLocalizedModule_comp_away ->
% lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_localizationAway -> lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover),
% now changing base to the coordinate ring S = Γ(V) of a general affine open instead of a single
% localisation R_f. The one genuinely-new Mathlib ingredient is the localised base-change comparison.

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Localised base change is a localisation away]
  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away}
  \uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_comp_away}
  \textit{Project-local infrastructure, assembled from Mathlib base-change primitives.}
  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a ring map, \(M\) an \(R\)-module, and \(g \in R\) with image
  \(\bar g = \varphi(g) \in S\). The base change \(M \otimes_R S\), further localised at the powers of
  \(\bar g\), is a localisation of \(M\): the composite
  \[
    M \;\longrightarrow\; M \otimes_R S \;\longrightarrow\; (M \otimes_R S)_{\bar g}
  \]
  exhibits \((M \otimes_R S)_{\bar g}\) as the base change of \(M\) along the composite
  \(R \to S \to S_{\bar g}\). In particular, whenever the localised ring \(S_{\bar g}\) is identified
  with the localisation \(R_g\) --- as happens when \(D_S(\bar g)\) and \(D_R(g)\) name the same basic
  open --- the composite presents \((M \otimes_R S)_{\bar g}\) as the away localisation \(M_g\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_comp_away}
  Tensoring with \(S\) exhibits \(M \otimes_R S\) as the base change of \(M\) along \(R \to S\)
  (\(\operatorname{TensorProduct.isBaseChange}\)); localising at the powers of \(\bar g\) exhibits
  \((M \otimes_R S)_{\bar g}\) as the base change of \(M \otimes_R S\) along \(S \to S_{\bar g}\)
  (\(\operatorname{isLocalizedModule\_iff\_isBaseChange}\)). Composing the two base changes
  (\(\operatorname{IsBaseChange.comp}\)) exhibits the composite \(M \to (M \otimes_R S)_{\bar g}\) as the
  base change of \(M\) along \(R \to S_{\bar g}\). Under the identification \(S_{\bar g} = R_g\) this is
  exactly the away localisation of \(M\) at \(g\); the two-step composite specialises to the localised
  module recipe already recorded in Lemma~\ref{lem:isLocalizedModule_comp_away}.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Section {\v C}ech module exactness from an affine-cover base-change datum]
  \label{lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_affineCover}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact_of_affineCover,
    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAbExact_affine}
  \uses{lem:section_cech_module_exact, lem:isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away}
  \textit{Project-local infrastructure; mirror of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_localizationAway}.}
  Let \(M\) be an \(R\)-module and \(g : \iota \to R\) a finite family whose union
  \(V = \bigcup_i D(g_i)\) is an affine open of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), with coordinate ring
  \(S = \Gamma(V, \mathcal{O}_V)\) and restriction map \(\varphi : R \to S\), \(\bar g_i = \varphi(g_i)\).
  Since the \(D_S(\bar g_i)\) cover \(\operatorname{Spec} S = V\), the images \(\bar g_i\) span the unit
  ideal of \(S\). Then the un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet(g, M)\) of
  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} is exact in every positive degree --- \emph{without}
  assuming that \(g\) spans the unit ideal of \(R\) itself.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:section_cech_module_exact, lem:isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away}
  \leanok
  Run the computation over \(S\). Instantiate the polymorphic positive-degree exactness of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} over the base ring \(S\), with module \(M \otimes_R S\) and
  the spanning family \(\bar g : \iota \to S\); spanning is exactly the affineness of the cover's union,
  so the \(S\)-side complex \(D^\bullet(\bar g, M \otimes_R S)\) is exact in positive degrees. Transport
  this exactness back to the \(R\)-side along the degreewise \(R\)-linear isomorphisms
  \(M_{g_\sigma} \cong (M \otimes_R S)_{\bar g_\sigma}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away} (for a tuple \(\sigma\),
  \(g_\sigma = \prod_k g_{\sigma k}\) and \(\bar g_\sigma = \varphi(g_\sigma)\), with
  \(\Gamma(D(g_\sigma))\) the common localisation). Realised through \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\),
  these comparisons are the vertical maps of a ladder, and exactness transfers across it by
  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact.of\_ladder\_addEquiv\_of\_exact}\), the cofaces commuting by naturality
  of the localisation comparison.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Tilde {\v C}ech vanishing over an affine-union standard cover]
  \label{lem:sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineCover}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineCover}
  \uses{lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_affineCover, lem:section_cech_ab_exact,
    lem:section_cech_homology_exact}
  \textit{Project-local infrastructure; mirror of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover}.}
  Let \(R\) be a ring, \(M\) an \(R\)-module, and \(g : \iota \to R\) a finite family whose union
  \(V = \bigcup_i D(g_i)\) is an affine open of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\). Then for every \(p > 0\) the
  homology of the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(\{D(g_i)\}\) vanishes,
  \[
    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\{D(g_i)\}, \widetilde{M}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr).
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_affineCover, lem:section_cech_ab_exact,
    lem:section_cech_homology_exact}
  \leanok
  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_affineCover} supplies positive-degree exactness of the
  un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet(g, M)\) from the affineness of the cover's
  union. The abelian-group exactness criterion (the private \(\operatorname{sectionCechAbExact\_affine}\)
  packaging of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}) transports this module exactness across the
  degreewise additive ladder identifying the section complex with the localised-module complex
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}), and reads the resulting function exactness off as the
  vanishing of the degree-\(p\) homology via
  \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_isZero\_homology\_of\_objD\_exact}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[The cover arrow is the coproduct of its member arrows in the slice]
  \label{lem:coverArrow_over_sigma}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverSigmaIso,
    AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverCofan,
    AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverIsColimit}
  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve}
  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an open cover with cover arrow
  \(q = \operatorname{Sigma.desc}\mathcal{U}.f : \coprod_{i \in I} U_i \to X\). Then in the slice
  category \(\operatorname{Over} X\) the object \(\operatorname{Over.mk}(q)\) carrying the cover arrow is
  the coproduct of the member arrows \(\operatorname{Over.mk}(\mathcal{U}.f\,i)\):
  \[
    \operatorname{Over.mk}\bigl(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\mathcal{U}.f\bigr)
      \;\cong\;
    \coprod_{i \in I} \operatorname{Over.mk}(\mathcal{U}.f\,i)
      \qquad\text{in } \operatorname{Over} X.
  \]
  This is the degree-\(0\) instance of the {\v C}ech backbone identification
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma}): the source of the cover arrow is the disjoint union of the
  cover members, structurally over \(X\).
\end{lemma}

  has apex \(\operatorname{Over.mk}(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\mathcal{U}.f)\), with the \(i\)-th leg the
  morphism in \(\operatorname{Over} X\) lifting the coproduct inclusion
  \(\iota_i : U_i \to \coprod_j U_j\) (well-defined as an arrow over \(X\) because
  \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\mathcal{U}.f \circ \iota_i = \mathcal{U}.f\,i\), the triangle
  \(\operatorname{Over.w}\)). It is a colimit (\(\operatorname{coverArrowOverIsColimit}\)) by

  \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\) of the components (\(\operatorname{Sigma.ι\_desc}\)), and uniqueness is

### TASK 1 — Decompose the Need#1 jShriekOU transport (Route-3 corrective)
Inserted six new blocks immediately before `lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport`, then rewrote that
lemma's statement `\lean{}`, `\uses{}`, and proof prose.

- **Added lemma** `\label{lem:pushforward_commutes_free}` / `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_commutes_free}`
  — pushforward along an iso commutes with the free functor (left-adjoint / mate argument).
  `\uses{def:jshriek_ou}`. Marked `% NOTE: build target`.
- **Added lemma** `\label{lem:pushforward_commutes_sheafify}` / `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_commutes_sheafify}`
  — pushforward along an iso commutes with sheafification (site equivalence / left adjoints).
  `\uses{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}`. `% NOTE: build target`.
- **Added lemma** `\label{lem:yoneda_transport_along_homeo}` / `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.yoneda_transport_along_homeo}`
  — representable transports to the representable on the image open (open-image order-iso). `% NOTE: build target`.
- **Added lemma** `\label{lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso}` / `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.jShriekOU_transport_along_iso}`
  — assembly: `Φ(j_!O_V) ≅ j_!O_{φ''V}` by chaining sheafify→free→Yoneda commutations.
  `\uses{def:jshriek_ou, lem:pushforward_commutes_free, lem:pushforward_commutes_sheafify, lem:yoneda_transport_along_homeo}`. `% NOTE: build target`.
- **Added lemma** `\label{lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh}` / `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh}`
  — pushforward along an iso preserves quasi-coherence (transport of local presentation along
  `ringCatSheaf` iso). `% NOTE: build target`.
- **Added Mathlib anchor** `\label{lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}` /
  `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion}` `\mathlibok`
  — image of an affine open under an open immersion (a fortiori iso) is affine.
- **Revised** `lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport`:
  - `\lean{}` now bundles the three construction internals
    `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso`,
    `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive`,
    `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv` alongside `modulesIsoSpecExtTransport`.
  - `\uses{}` (statement + proof): dropped stale `lem:jshriek_corepr`; added
    `lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso, lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh, lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib`.
  - Proof prose rewritten: the single "Yoneda corepresentability carries along" sentence is replaced by
    explicit appeals to `\ref{lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso}` (object transport),
    `\ref{lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}` (affineness of `V'`), and
    `\ref{lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh}` (qcoh preservation); the prose now names the
    `pushforwardEquivOfIso` / `pushforwardExtAddEquiv` / `mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects`
    internals.

### TASK 2 — Clear coverage debt
**CechAcyclic change-of-ring chain** (inserted before the seed block `lem:affine_cech_vanishing_general_seed`):
- **Added lemma** `\label{lem:isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away}` /
  `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away}` — the genuinely-new Mathlib ingredient
  (TensorProduct.isBaseChange + isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange + IsBaseChange.comp).
  `\uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_comp_away}`.
- **Added lemma** `\label{lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_affineCover}` —
  `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact_of_affineCover, AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAbExact_affine}`
  (private helper `sectionCechAbExact_affine` bundled in, per directive). Mirror of
  `lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_localizationAway`.
  `\uses{lem:section_cech_module_exact, lem:isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away}`.
- **Added lemma** `\label{lem:sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineCover}` /
  `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineCover}`. Mirror of
  `lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover`.
  `\uses{lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_affineCover, lem:section_cech_ab_exact, lem:section_cech_homology_exact}`
  (the latter two carry `sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact`).
- **Revised** the seed block `lem:affine_cech_vanishing_general_seed`: confirmed its `\lean{}` already
  points at `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen` (the repoint was already in
  place); bundled the private helper `AlgebraicGeometry.basicOpen_algMap_section` into its `\lean{}`;
  added `lem:sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineCover, lem:isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away` to both
  `\uses{}` lists; updated the stale `% NOTE` (was "is to be built") to reflect it is co-located/built;
  amended the Conclusion paragraph to cite the two new core lemmas.

**CechSectionIdentification coproduct leaf** (inserted after `lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma`):
- **Added lemma** `\label{lem:coverArrow_over_sigma}` —
  `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverSigmaIso, AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverCofan, AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverIsColimit}`
  (the two cofan/colimit helpers bundled as implementation details). The cover arrow `Over.mk(Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)`
  is the coproduct of member arrows in `Over X`. `\uses{def:cover_cech_nerve}`; prose names
  mkCofanColimit / coproductIsCoproduct / Sigma.{ι_desc,hom_ext} / Over.{homMk,w}.

**OpenImmersionPushforward transport core** — the three names
`pushforwardEquivOfIso`, `pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive`, `pushforwardExtAddEquiv` were bundled
into `lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport`'s `\lean{}` (see TASK 1 revision above) and are mentioned in
its proof prose.

### TASK 3 — Repoint the Need#2 consumer pin
- **Revised** `lem:affine_serre_vanishing_general_open`: `\lean{}` repointed from
  `...affine_serre_vanishing_general_open_TODO` to the real

  exist yet` comment. Existing seed wiring (`lem:affine_cech_vanishing_general_seed`) left intact.

## Cross-references introduced
- All new `\uses{}` targets verified present in this chapter via `leandag build` → `unknown_uses: []`

- `unknown_uses: []` — no dangling references.

- **Minor directive label fix.** The directive specified `lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso` should
